slice: Locked the Platform Representation Contract: a Theme MUST have >=1 valid Representation (only structural requirement), SHOULD have more when it helps, CAN use any names -- the platform never reserves one. Found and fixed a real double-counting bug: every World Builder template still carried a static legacy representations/*.json array from before Scenes existed, ON TOP of the starter Scene that already converges into one Representation at Build -- every template compiled 2-4 Representations, never the intended baseline. Removed every template's static representations array; the starter Scene is now the sole default. Artwork Gallery's starter Scene renamed Showcase -> Story (the sprint's suggested neutral default); Cover/Quote/Sketch/Card left unchanged (never part of the flagged set). validator.js now requires >=1 Representation from either representations/ or scenes/, with no name/count-above-one requirement. Studio required zero changes (already fully data-driven). Museum Gallery's showcase/portrait/quote verified unchanged -- no migration. New 15-assertion Playwright suite plus goldenBuild.js 30/30, Happy Flow suite, and WEP scope suite all pass unchanged
